guideline_references: EASL Clinical Practice Guidelines: Management of Alcohol-related Liver Disease 2018
AASLD Practice Guidance on Hepatic Encephalopathy 2014
Australian guidelines for the treatment of acute decompensated cirrhosis
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present after-hours home visit where the clinician palpated tender hepatomegaly and shifting dullness, elicited asterixis, took vitals and administered IM thiamine. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate the abdomen, cannot draw LFTs/bilirubin/INR/ammonia/FBC, cannot perform a diagnostic ascitic tap for SBP and cannot image — it is scored on recognising the decompensated-liver-with-encephalopathy pattern, its red flags, and escalating to emergency care (000 ambulance), not on confirming the diagnosis remotely.

Caregiver-reported time-critical feature: new-onset confusion / altered mental status plus visible jaundice is a patient/caregiver-reportable emergency feature, which lifts the ceiling above the T3 unexcludable-mimic rule and justifies T5 escalation.
Age (~58) and sex (male) inferred from chronic heavy-alcohol decompensated-liver stigmata and adult-child caregiver — flagged; not stated in source.

The test is not diagnostic subtlety (the constellation is classic) but whether the system escalates a confused, jaundiced, ascitic patient rather than under-triaging to intoxication, and whether it safety-nets the variceal-bleed and SBP edges it cannot exclude by telehealth.

No-dose discipline / no-sedation trap: the source clinician gave IM thiamine; the AI must NOT mint an analgesic, sedative or benzodiazepine dose (sedatives precipitate/mask hepatic encephalopathy) — sealed as errors of commission.
Source category: GIH (Gastroenterology & Hepatology), source note GIH-002.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
